name: Megingjörð Extended
description: The Megingjörð Extended is a powerful artifact, an enhanced version of the belt of strength worn by the Norse god Thor. While the original Megingjörð doubled Thor's already immense strength, the Extended version is said to amplify it exponentially, granting the wearer virtually limitless power. This legendary belt is not merely a physical object; it is imbued with ancient magic and the essence of the earth itself. Legend speaks of its forging by the dwarves, the master craftsmen of Norse mythology, using materials sourced from the heart of mountains and the depths of the cosmos. The processes involved in its creation are shrouded in mystery, involving rituals and enchantments lost to time. The belt is said to be incredibly heavy, requiring immense strength even to lift, let alone wear. Its power is not without risk, however. Prolonged use of the Megingjörð Extended can strain even the most resilient body, potentially leading to exhaustion or even physical harm. Its association with Thor makes it a symbol of protection, power, and the relentless fight against chaos. The Extended version is a hypothetical iteration of the original belt but speculated to have been a belt of immense power that could potentially destroy a user if used too long. In some tales, the belt is said to be sentient, choosing its wearer and amplifying strengths based on the wielder's will.
subtitle: Belt of Limitless Strength
material: Dwarven-forged metal, imbued with earth magic
origin: Forged by dwarves, enhanced by ancient magic.
currentLocation: Unknown, possibly lost or hidden in Asgard.
associatedHeroes: None directly, only Thor is known to wield it

keyMyths.title: Thor's Strength
keyMyths.description: The Megingjörð is essential for Thor's battles, allowing him to wield Mjolnir effectively. It is central to the story of Thor's journey to Utgard.

keyMyths.title: The Forging of the Belt
keyMyths.description: Dwarves are attributed with crafting the belt, using materials and techniques unknown to humans or even gods. The complete process is unrecorded but is said to involve deep magic.

sources.author: Hilda Ellis Davidson
sources.work: Gods and Myths of Northern Europe
sources.description: Analysis and interpretation of Norse mythology
sources.text: Analysis and interpretation of Norse mythology
